# Nim UI - Quick Reference for AI

> Quiet, accessible React UI kit for dashboards, backoffice, and commerce operations

## Stack

- React 19.2+, TypeScript 5.9+ (strict), Tailwind CSS v4 (OKLCH tokens)
- Build: tsup + PostCSS, Turborepo, pnpm 9+
- Test: Vitest, React Testing Library (coverage gates 80/75/70/80)
- Docs: Next.js 16 + Fumadocs 16 (static export) at https://nim-ui.tiinno.com

## Project Structure

```
nim-ui/
├── packages/
│   ├── ui/               # Component library (@nim-ui/components)
│   │   └── src/
│   │       ├── components/   # All components (flat, kebab-case files)
│   │       ├── lib/          # cn (clsx), date-format
│   │       ├── registry/     # index.json — machine-readable component registry
│   │       └── tokens.css    # OKLCH design tokens (single source of truth)
│   ├── docs/             # Fumadocs documentation site
│   ├── tailwind-config/  # Shared Tailwind config
│   └── mcp-server/       # MCP server (reads the registry)
└── turbo.json
```

## 85 Components (8 Categories)

### Primitives (10)
- Button: A versatile button component with multiple variants and sizes for different use cases
- Input: A flexible input field component with validation states and multiple sizes
- Select: A dropdown select component with search and multi-select capabilities, built with Radix UI
- Checkbox: A checkbox input component with label and indeterminate state support, built with Radix UI
- Radio: A radio button component for selecting a single option from a group, built with Radix UI
- Textarea: A textarea component with validation states and sizes for multi-line text input
- Switch: A toggle switch component for boolean on/off states, built with Radix UI
- Text: Typography primitive rendering semantic text elements with a compact operational type scale, tone, and weight control
- Kbd: Keyboard key display with KbdGroup for shortcut combinations, styled to match CommandMenu shortcuts
- ButtonGroup: Attached button container that collapses inner radii and borders into a single segmented control

### Layout (15)
- Card: A container component for grouping related content with header, body, and footer sections
- AdminShell: A backoffice application frame with sidebar, topbar, panel, and main content landmarks
- PageHeader: A backoffice page header with title, description, metadata, and grouped actions
- Modal: A dialog/modal component for displaying content in an overlay, built with Radix UI
- Drawer: A slide-out panel component for side navigation or additional content, built with Radix UI
- Tabs: A tabbed interface component for organizing content into separate views, built with Radix UI
- Container: A responsive container component with max-width constraints and optional padding
- Grid: A responsive CSS Grid layout component with configurable columns and gap
- Stack: A stack component for vertical or horizontal stacking with consistent spacing
- Flex: A flexible flexbox layout component for common alignment and distribution patterns
- Spacer: A spacer component for adding fixed or flexible spacing between elements
- Accordion: A collapsible content component for organizing information into expandable sections, built with Radix UI
- Separator: A visual divider component for separating content sections, built with Radix UI
- Collapsible: An expandable section for hiding and revealing content, built with Radix UI
- ScrollArea: Radix scroll container with quiet themed scrollbars, native keyboard scrolling preserved

### Data Display (18)
- Badge: A small badge component for displaying status, counts, or labels
- StatusPill: A compact operational state pill for admin tables, orders, payments, and user workflows
- FilterSummary: A compact active-filter summary with removable chips and clear-all behavior for search-heavy admin screens
- Avatar: A user avatar component with image fallback and initials support, built with Radix UI
- DataCard: A specialized card for displaying data with labels, values, and optional trends
- MetricCard: A dashboard KPI card with value, trend, tone, supporting text, visual slot, and footer metadata
- DataTable: A table component for displaying structured data with sorting and styling options
- DataToolbar: A table and list toolbar for search, filters, result metadata, and actions
- BulkActionBar: A sticky selected-row action bar for admin tables and bulk workflows
- EmptyState: An empty table and list state with title, description, decorative icon, and recovery actions
- Timeline: A semantic activity history component for audit trails, order events, and status changes
- DescriptionList: A semantic label-value metadata component for detail panels, drawers, and record pages
- RecordInspector: A compact record detail panel for backoffice review, metadata, actions, and activity
- Stat: A component for displaying statistics with labels, values, and optional trend indicators
- Dot: Minimal inline status dot with optional label for dense table cells and list rows
- Meter: Capacity and usage bar with automatic threshold coloring, distinct from Progress task completion
- CopyButton: Ghost icon button that copies a value to the clipboard with copied feedback and live-region announcement
- Snippet: Copyable mono field for record IDs, API keys, and webhook URLs with built-in CopyButton and display-only prefix

### Commerce (4)
- ProductCard: A card component optimized for displaying product information including images, titles, and prices
- CartItem: A component for displaying shopping cart items with quantity controls and removal options
- PriceTag: A component for displaying prices with optional original price and discount information
- QuantitySelector: A component for selecting quantities with increment and decrement buttons

### Landing (6)
- Navbar: A responsive top navigation bar for marketing pages with brand, links, CTA, and mobile menu
- Hero: A hero section component for landing pages with headline, description, and call-to-action
- FeatureGrid: A grid layout component for displaying features with icons, titles, and descriptions
- CTA: A call-to-action component for encouraging user actions with headlines and buttons
- Testimonial: A component for displaying customer testimonials with quotes, names, and avatars
- Footer: A marketing site footer with brand, link groups, social links, legal links, and copyright

### Forms (17)
- FormLayout: Sectioned CRUD form layout primitives for backoffice create and edit workflows
- Form: A form wrapper component with built-in validation and submission handling
- FormField: A form field component with label, input, error message, and helper text support
- Slider: A slider input for selecting a value or range within a bounded interval, built with Radix UI
- Combobox: A searchable, filterable dropdown input built on cmdk and Radix Popover
- Calendar: A calendar component for date selection, built on react-day-picker v9
- DatePicker: An input trigger with a calendar popover for selecting dates, composing Popover, Calendar, and Button
- TimePicker: A compact popover time selector for operational forms and scheduling controls
- DateTimePicker: Date and time pickers for scheduling, windows, publish times, and backoffice cutoffs
- DateFilter: A date-range filter with operational presets for tables, reports, and dashboards
- MultiSelect: A searchable multi-select with removable chips and hidden inputs for native forms
- TagsInput: A tag entry field for labels, segments, and operational metadata
- FileUpload: A local file picker and dropzone for attachment selection without upload transport
- NumberInput: A numeric input with optional steppers, clamping, and precision handling
- PasswordInput: A password input with accessible show and hide controls
- Stepper: A compact stepper for multi-step forms, setup flows, and review workflows
- Fieldset: Semantic fieldset/legend form section with description, content, and footer areas; native disabled propagation

### Feedback (9)
- Popover: Displays rich content in a floating panel triggered by a click, built with Radix UI
- Tooltip: Displays informational text when hovering or focusing on an element, built with Radix UI
- DropdownMenu: A dropdown menu component for displaying actions or options triggered by a button click, built with Radix UI
- AlertDialog: A modal dialog for confirming destructive or important actions that requires explicit user response, built with Radix UI
- Toast: Lightweight notification messages with declarative components and an imperative toast API
- Alert: An inline feedback message component for displaying contextual alerts with dismiss support
- Progress: A progress bar component for displaying completion percentage with multiple variants
- Spinner: An animated loading spinner indicator with multiple sizes and color variants
- Skeleton: A placeholder loading component with pulse animation for content that is loading

### Navigation (6)
- Breadcrumb: A navigation component for displaying the current page location within a hierarchical structure
- CommandMenu: Searchable command surface for backoffice navigation, record lookup, and operator actions
- ViewSwitcher: A saved-view and operational-queue switcher for backoffice tables and dashboards
- SidebarNav: Grouped sidebar navigation with active states, badges, and icon slots for admin workspaces
- Pagination: Composable pagination building blocks for navigating through pages of content
- Link: Styled anchor with muted steel accent, subtle and standalone variants, and safe external-link handling

## Commands

```bash
pnpm install                          # Install deps
pnpm dev                              # Start dev servers
pnpm build                            # Build all packages
pnpm --filter @nim-ui/components test # Run UI tests (vitest watch mode; use npx vitest run for one-shot)
pnpm lint                             # Lint code
pnpm --filter docs build              # Build docs (static export to packages/docs/out)
```

## Component Pattern (CVA)

```tsx
import * as React from 'react';
import { cva, type VariantProps } from 'class-variance-authority';
import { cn } from '../lib/utils';

const badgeVariants = cva('base-classes', {
  variants: {
    variant: { default: 'classes', success: 'classes' },
    size: { sm: 'classes', md: 'classes' },
  },
  defaultVariants: { variant: 'default', size: 'md' },
});

export interface BadgeProps
  extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>,
    VariantProps<typeof badgeVariants> {}

const Badge = React.forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, BadgeProps>(
  ({ className, variant, size, ...props }, ref) => (
    <div className={cn(badgeVariants({ variant, size }), className)} ref={ref} {...props} />
  )
);
Badge.displayName = 'Badge';

export { Badge, badgeVariants };
```

Note: `cn` is plain clsx (no tailwind-merge) — never rely on later classes overriding earlier ones.

## Design Language

- Premium Operations Minimal: calm hierarchy, restrained color, dense-but-readable
- Ink + Muted Steel Accent: graphite/ink primary actions; the primary-* scale is a subdued steel blue-gray for focus, selection, links
- Compact Premium Density: 8px component radii, quiet borders, soft layered shadow (shadow-control)

## Design Tokens (tokens.css, OKLCH)

- Color scales: primary, neutral, success, error, warning, info — each 50…950 (bg-success-500, text-error-700, …)
- Dark mode: class strategy — pair every color with its dark: variant inline
- Radius: rounded-md = 0.5rem; animation: duration-fast/duration-slow + animate-* keyframes

## Accessibility Checklist

- ARIA attributes (aria-label, aria-describedby, role)
- Keyboard navigation (Enter, Space, Escape, Arrows)
- Focus management (focus-visible:ring-2 ring-primary-400)
- Screen reader support (aria-live, sr-only announcements)
- Semantic HTML first (button, fieldset/legend, kbd, a)

## Testing Pattern

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
import { render, screen } from '../test/test-utils';
import { Badge, badgeVariants } from './badge';

it('renders variant classes incl. dark mode', () => {
  render(<Badge data-testid="badge" variant="success">Active</Badge>);
  expect(screen.getByTestId('badge')).toHaveClass('bg-success-50');
});
// Tests assert exact class strings, ref forwarding, *Variants() output, aria attributes
```

## MCP Server (5 Tools)

Located: `/packages/mcp-server/` — serves `packages/ui/src/registry/index.json`

1. **ui_list_components**: all components by category
2. **ui_get_component**: detailed info for one component
3. **ui_get_tokens**: design system tokens
4. **ui_search_components**: find components by query/category
5. **ui_get_example**: usage examples

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "nim-ui": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["path/to/nim-ui/packages/mcp-server/dist/index.js"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Key Rules

1. ALWAYS use CVA for variants and export the *Variants function
2. ALWAYS use forwardRef + displayName
3. ALWAYS pair light classes with dark: variants inline
4. NEVER use inline styles (Tailwind only; width-from-value via style only for meters/progress)
5. ALWAYS add ARIA attributes and semantic elements
6. ALWAYS write co-located tests (coverage ≥ 80/75/70/80)
7. ALWAYS export from src/components/index.ts AND add a registry entry in src/registry/index.json
8. New docs page: packages/docs/content/docs/components/<category>/<name>.mdx + category meta.json

## Common Imports

```tsx
import { Button, DataTable, StatusPill, Snippet } from '@nim-ui/components';
import '@nim-ui/components/styles';
```

## Quality Gates

Before commit:

```bash
pnpm lint && pnpm --filter @nim-ui/components build && npx vitest run
```

All must pass.

---

**Updated**: 2026-07-15 | **Tech**: React 19 + TS 5.9 + Tailwind v4 | 85 components / 8 categories
